item.title: The Future - The Last Developer Standing
item.description:
Current state of AI-assisted development (GitHub Copilot, Claude Code, autonomous agents). Demonstrations of agentic coding workflows. Argues developers who think clearest (not type fastest) will thrive with AI tools.

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held at Beach Walk Coworking, 96 Beach Walk Blvd, Conroe, TX 77304 in the ground floor meeting room. Free parking is available on site.

item.title: From CI/CD to Continuous Scheduling: The Smart Repo Revolution
item.description:
Modern AI coding tools are powerful, but without structure, they’re chaotic. In this session, Mark Wardell introduces Repo-Driven Development (RDD), a methodology that transforms your Git repository into the single source of truth for AI-assisted development: a living project manager, a deterministic prompt engine, and a scheduling system, all in one place.

Mark will demonstrate the Smart Repo VSCode Extension, a practical tool that brings RDD to life by giving developers structured control over what their LLM codes, in what order, and under what constraints. We’ll explore how well-crafted GitLab issues, epics, and pipelines become the guardrails that turn unpredictable AI output into production-ready code, and how this pattern pushes teams beyond CI/CD into a new paradigm: Continuous Scheduling (CS), where development workflows are no longer triggered by commits alone, but by time, context, and intent.

Whether you’re a solo developer or leading a team, you’ll leave with a concrete framework for making AI work for your process, not against it.

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held at Beach Walk Coworking, 96 Beach Walk Blvd, Conroe, TX 77304 in the ground floor meeting room. Free parking is available on site.

item.title: Should You Build an MCP Server?
item.description:
Building robust and secure software tools in era of LLM chat interfaces replacing traditional web apps. Discussion of how developers can continue delivering quality tools as interface paradigms shift.

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held at Beach Walk Coworking, 96 Beach Walk Blvd, Conroe, TX 77304 in the ground floor meeting room. Free parking is available on site.

item.title: Lightning Talks!
item.description:
We are doing something a little different for our April 2026 meetup: Lightning Talks⚡⚡⚡!

Two speakers, 15-20 minutes each.

Our Lightning Talk Lineup

Mutant RALPH: Squeezing LLM Code with Adversarial Mutation Testing

Speaker: Peter Hellmuth, Senior Full Stack Developer at Logisolve

What if your AI coding agent had an adversary? A systems engineering experiment with a bash-orchestrated loop pitting a Writer agent against an Adversary using mutation testing (Stryker.NET). Identifies gaps in requirement enforcement, forces resolution of ambiguities through deterministic mutant killing.

Level: Intermediate Topics: C#, Architecture, Testing, Patterns & Best Practices, AI/ML

The Anatomy of a Smart Repo: Repo-Driven Development in 15 Minutes

Speaker: Mark Wardell, Founder at The Middle Way Software

You’ve got GitHub Copilot, Claude, or Cursor — and your AI still wanders. Mark breaks down Repo-Driven Development (RDD): a lightweight methodology that turns your Git repository into an intelligent project manager for AI-assisted coding. In 15 minutes you’ll see what makes a repo “smart” — structured issues, intentional epics, and pipeline conventions that tell your LLM what to build, in what order, and within what guardrails.

Level: Intermediate Topics: Architecture, AI/ML, Career Development

If this sounds like fun, please submit our speaker interest form here: https://forms.gle/aVzeVpvVhAkdfWLy7

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held in the offices of SmartDraw Software, 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 on the 11th floor. Free parking is available in the adjacent garage.

item.title: Software 2.0: It's Not Vibe Coding, But It's Not Business as Usual Either
item.description:
How is AI-assisted development fundamentally shifting the way we verify software?

In this talk, Aaron explores how the rise of AI-powered development tools is changing the game for software engineers. This isn’t “vibe coding” — it’s a real shift in how we think about building and verifying software systems. Key topics include:

  1. Prompt engineering effectiveness: how to get the most out of AI-assisted development tools.
  2. Designing feedback mechanisms: building systems that catch AI errors before they reach production.
  3. Blast radius planning: managing risk when your development process involves probabilistic outputs rather than deterministic ones.

Modern development increasingly involves building systems whose behavior is probabilistic rather than deterministic — and that requires new approaches to verification, testing, and risk management.

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held in the offices of SmartDraw Software, 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 on the 11th floor. Free parking is available in the adjacent garage.

item.title: .NET Conf 2025 & NHDNUG Holiday Party
item.description:
Join NHDNUG for our 2025 Holiday Party and a live .NET Conf 2025 watch party at the SmartDraw Software offices! We’re treating all registered attendees to a delicious meal from Postino, so bring your appetite and your love for .NET!

We’ll be recapping and replaying the best moments from .NET Conf, which aired live from November 11-13. Come enjoy great food, exciting .NET news, and festive networking with fellow members. It’s the perfect way to celebrate the season!

Food / Drinks

NHDNUG will cover the cost of your meal and drinks courtesy of Postino. If you have any dietary restrictions, please let us know in advance so we can accommodate you.

Directions and Parking Instructions

We’ll be meeting on the 11th floor of SmartDraw Software offices at 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 in The Woodlands. Free parking is available in the nearby garage.

Looking for Speakers in 2026!

We’re actively seeking speakers for our 2026 meetups! If you’re interested in presenting, please contact us at hi@petabridge.com or complete our speaker interest form.

item.title: Is Your Local Dev Environment a Mess? How .NET Aspire Can Help [Take 2]
item.description:
UPDATE: welp, our October meetup got abruptly cancelled mid-talk due to building management deciding to run a prolonged fire alarm test beginning at 6pm. So, we’ve scheduled for November!

If you follow any of Microsoft’s big personalities on social media you might get the impression that .NET Aspire was curing cancer, proving P=NP, and on the verge of delivering sustainable nuclear fusion power.

It’s not, not even close.

But it is a tremendous improvement in developer productivity for local development, integration testing… and maybe one day in the future, deployments.

In this talk we’re going to use .NET Aspire to:

  1. Streamline local development: wire up your applications along with all of its real dependencies, so F5 ”just works” and launches a full, ready-to-debug instance of your app without any shared databases or other resources.
  2. Integration test without mocks: why use fake testing utilities when you can just run your real application and really test against it using Aspire.Hosting.Testing?
  3. Experiment with local deployments: the aspire CLI and the deployment model are the most experimental parts of Aspire, but we’re going to look at them and see what the future holds.

.NET Aspire can help get rid of the awful rats’ nest of shared databases, missing functionality, and configuration hell that most production code bases settle for - and instead deliver a git clone > dotnet run experience out of the box.

Code Samples

  • https://github.com/petabridge/DrawTogether.NET
  • https://github.com/akkadotnet/Akka.Streams.Kafka/tree/dev/examples/Simple

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held in the offices of SmartDraw Software, 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 on the 11th floor.

item.title: Is Your Local Dev Environment a Mess? How .NET Aspire Can Help
item.description:
If you follow any of Microsoft’s big personalities on social media you might get the impression that .NET Aspire was curing cancer, proving P=NP, and on the verge of delivering sustainable nuclear fusion power.

It’s not, not even close.

But it is a tremendous improvement in developer productivity for local development, integration testing… and maybe one day in the future, deployments.

In this talk we’re going to use .NET Aspire to:

  1. Streamline local development: wire up your applications along with all of its real dependencies, so F5 ”just works” and launches a full, ready-to-debug instance of your app without any shared databases or other resources.
  2. Integration test without mocks: why use fake testing utilities when you can just run your real application and really test against it using Aspire.Hosting.Testing?
  3. Experiment with local deployments: the aspire CLI and the deployment model are the most experimental parts of Aspire, but we’re going to look at them and see what the future holds.

.NET Aspire can help get rid of the awful rats’ nest of shared databases, missing functionality, and configuration hell that most production code bases settle for - and instead deliver a git clone > dotnet run experience out of the box.

Code Samples

  • https://github.com/petabridge/DrawTogether.NET
  • https://github.com/akkadotnet/Akka.Streams.Kafka/tree/dev/examples/Simple

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held in the offices of SmartDraw Software, 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 on the 11th floor.

item.title: Eight Reasons to Try F#
item.description:
Many F# introductions follow the same pattern: a dutiful introduction to syntax, a few allusions to functional programming, a “Hello World” code sample. I’m not sure these presentations have ever convinced anyone to try F#.

In this talk, I will build (…and break) an application to show off the killer language features that keep me coming back to .NET’s other language.

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held in the offices of SmartDraw Software, 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 on the 11th floor.

item.title: Leading from the IDE: How .NET Devs Can Rehearse Leadership with AI
item.description:
You’ve been building with .NET long enough to know what good code looks like. But leadership isn’t just about writing code—it’s about helping others write it well, too.

In this session, you’ll learn how to use AI to simulate team leadership: think pairing with a junior, refining specs, or documenting your thought process. Whether you lead a team or want to grow into the role, AI gives you a low-stakes way to practice the habits that make great leads.

Food / Drinks

Food and drinks will be provided! Please contact us (on meetup) if you have any dietary restrictions/food allergies.

Directions and Parking Instructions

Meeting will be held in the offices of SmartDraw Software, 1780 Hughes Landing Blvd #1100 on the 11th floor.
